heroHeadline: Min Aung Hlaing Inaugurated as President (April 10) — China and India Attend as SAC Bombs IDP Camp and Continues Sagaing Air Campaign
heroSubtitle: Myanmar junta chief Min Aung Hlaing was inaugurated as the country's 11th president on April 10, with China sending a special envoy and India dispatching its Minister of State for External Affairs — conferring regional legitimacy on the junta's cosmetic civilianization project condemned by the NUG, UN, US, and EU. On April 7, SAC jets conducted seven bombing runs on an IDP displacement camp in Shwebo Township (Sagaing), killing a monk teaching displaced children. Sustained fighting in Minbu-Saku (Magway) entered its sixth day with schools bombed; resistance ambushes in Ye and Mudon townships (Mon State) killed four SAC troops. The KIA's Hpakant offensive continued into April 8, injuring six civilians from artillery shelling, with multiple roads closed. On April 9, SAC airstrikes continued against Wuntho Township villages in northern Sagaing while the junta prepared its presidential inauguration — a stark contrast between the regime's nominal civilian transition and its ongoing military operations against civilians.
footerNote: Data sourced from AAPP, ACLED, UN OCHA, Irrawaddy, RFA, Myanmar Now, DVB, Al Jazeera, Mizzima, Human Rights Watch, Crisis Group, Myanmar Peace Monitor, MoeMaKa, and official NUG, KNU, KIA, and AA statements.
